questions.q: Which region on Earth experiences nearly uninterrupted daylight during the spring season?
What visible change typically occurs in gardens and forests as spring progresses?
choices: New plant growth and budding appear

questions.q: What happens to ground and air temperatures in spring climates that are free of snow?

choices: The first full moon of March

questions.q: During spring in the Northern Hemisphere, severe weather systems develop when cold Arctic air collides with warm air masses moving northward. What is the primary region where this conflict creates the most violent tornadoes?
choices: The Great Plains of North America

questions.q: The Sinhala New Year, which marks the beginning of spring in Sri Lanka, is celebrated in which month?

choices: The vernal equinox of the next year

questions.q: The day length increases from the vernal equinox to the summer solstice. By the summer solstice in the Northern Hemisphere, how many days of additional daylight have accumulated compared to the vernal equinox?
choices: Varying amounts depending on the year
Approximately 6 hours more per day at mid-northern latitudes

questions.q: In Celtic tradition, the spring festival of Beltane marked the beginning of summer, not spring. On which date was Beltane traditionally celebrated?
Easter's date varies each year because it is calculated based on which astronomical and lunar event?
choices: The first full moon following the vernal equinox
The second new moon after the winter solstice
The nearest full moon to the vernal equinox

questions.q: In spring, rapidly warming ground temperatures in previously snow-covered regions cause what type of air mass interaction that can produce severe weather?
choices: Cold air advection over warm ground
Warm air advection over cold ground

questions.q: The spring flowering sequence in temperate regions is regulated by environmental cues. Which of the following is NOT a primary trigger for spring flowering in most temperate plants?

Creating microclimates with delayed spring arrival
Absorbing solar radiation and storing heat
Acting as a barrier that influences the movement of air masses

questions.q: Ugadi, celebrated as the new year during spring in which Indian state, marks the beginning of the Hindu lunar calendar year?

During spring in North America, Tornado Alley experiences increased severe weather activity primarily due to colliding air masses. Which geographic region is known as Tornado Alley?
choices: The Great Plains and lower Mississippi Valley

questions.q: The increasing photoperiod during spring stimulates plant growth through the influence of which plant hormone?

Solar radiation reaches the surface at a higher angle, increasing intensity
Solar radiation becomes scattered and less intense
Solar radiation wavelengths shift toward infrared
